paper_title: Study of patients allergic to grass pollen undergoing AIT

methods.description: Procedure for recruiting patients with seasonal allergic rhinitis, obtaining informed consent, characterizing the study cohort, and collecting blood samples from patients and healthy controls at specific time points relative to AIT treatment.

steps.description: The study patient group included 22 consecutive patients allergic to grass pollen (6 women and 16 men) with diagnosis of seasonal allergic rhinitis.
steps.searchable_summary: Recruit patients meeting specific inclusion criteria (grass pollen allergy, seasonal allergic rhinitis).
inputs: Inclusion criteria (grass pollen allergy, seasonal allergic rhinitis)

steps.description: The severity was assessed according to the Allergic Rhinitis and its Impact on Asthma guidelines (Table 1) [27].
steps.searchable_summary: Assess allergic rhinitis severity using ARIA guidelines.

steps.description: All patients were recruited at the start of 3-year grass pollen subcutaneous AIT following the conventional protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Recruit patients who are starting subcutaneous AIT for grass pollen allergy.
outputs: Patients enrolled in AIT study arm

steps.description: Written, informed consent was obtained on enrolment.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain written informed consent from all participants.

steps.description: Blood samples were collected (using Tempus™ blood RNA tube; Applied Biosystems ® ) at two different time points: before starting the AIT and after reaching the maintenance dose (sample A and B, respectively).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect blood samples from patients using Tempus tubes at two time points: pre-AIT and post-AIT maintenance dose.
inputs: Time points (before AIT, after maintenance dose)
outputs: Patient blood samples (Sample A, Sample B)

steps.description: Blood collection before AIT was performed between August and May of the following year, which is outside the grass pollen season in northern Poland.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure pre-AIT blood sample collection occurs outside the local grass pollen season.

steps.description: Samples C were obtained at the same time as samples A from 25 healthy donors with a negative allergy history and negative grass pollen specific IgE.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect blood samples from healthy donors (controls) at the same time as patient Sample A.
inputs: Healthy donors (negative allergy history, negative grass pollen specific IgE)

steps.description: The mean age of participants was 31 years (range: 18-41). All patients were diagnosed with allergic rhinitis and 15 (68%) were additionally asthmatic (with a level of treatment according to Global Initiative for Asthma guidelines - Table 1) [28]. The most prevalent comorbidities were hypertension (3 patients) and smoking addiction (3 patients). The patient characteristics are presented in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Record and present patient demographic and clinical characteristics (age, asthma status, comorbidities).

methods.description: Procedure for evaluating the clinical efficacy of AIT treatment in patients using the Allergy Control Score (ACS) at two time points.

steps.description: All patients were evaluated for clinical treatment efficacy using the Allergy Control Score (ACS) based on non-commercial agreement with Allergo-Pharma.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate clinical treatment efficacy using the Allergy Control Score (ACS).

steps.description: To assess symptoms during two consecutive pollen seasons the patients underwent ACS scoring before AIT and after 1 year of treatment [29].
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ACS scoring at two time points: before AIT and after 1 year of treatment.
inputs: Time points (before AIT, after 1 year of treatment)
outputs: ACS scores at two time points

methods.description: Laboratory workflow for processing blood samples, isolating total mRNA, performing reverse transcription to synthesize cDNA, and conducting quantitative PCR using TaqMan array microfluidic cards to measure the expression levels of specific genes, followed by data normalization and calculation of Relative Quantification (RQ).

steps.description: All blood samples were stored at -80°C before processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Store collected blood samples at -80°C.

steps.description: Isolated mRNA was used to perform reverse transcription with Tempus ™ Spin RNA Isolation Kit (Applied Biosystems ® ). Isolated total mRNA was transferred to perform reverse transcription with High Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit (Applied Biosystems ® ) in Thermal Cycler 9700 (Applied Biosystems ® ).
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate total mRNA from blood samples using a kit.

steps.description: Isolated total mRNA was transferred to perform reverse transcription with High Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit (Applied Biosystems ® ) in Thermal Cycler 9700 (Applied Biosystems ® ).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform reverse transcription of isolated mRNA to synthesize cDNA using a kit and thermal cycler.
inputs: High Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit
keywords: High Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit

steps.description: 450 ng Universal Human Reference RNA (Agilent Technologies) was used as a control.
steps.searchable_summary: Include Universal Human Reference RNA as a control during the reverse transcription reaction.
inputs: High Capacity cDNA Reverse Transcription Kit reaction components

steps.description: The cDNA product was then transferred to TaqMan Array Micro Fluidic Cards (Applied Biosystems ® ) for PCR in 7900HT Real Time Fast PCR System (Applied Biosystems ® ).
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer cDNA product to TaqMan Array Micro Fluidic Cards for subsequent PCR.
outputs: Prepared TaqMan cards loaded with cDNA

steps.description: for PCR in 7900HT Real Time Fast PCR System (Applied Biosystems ® ). The microfluidic cards had 16 probes placed by the manufacturer for detection of the following genes: AFAP1L1, CLDN1, COMMD8, FCER1A, FOXP3, GATA3, IFNG, INPP5D, PCDHB10, PIK3CD, PRLR, SYK, TBX21, TGFB, TWIST2, and 18S. 18S ribosomal rRNA was used as a housekeeping gene for normalization.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform real-time PCR using TaqMan array cards targeting specific genes and a housekeeping gene (18S) on a real-time PCR system.
inputs: List of target genes and housekeeping gene (18S)
outputs: Raw real-time PCR data (fluorescence readings over cycles)

steps.description: The result of PCR was expressed as a number of cycles until cDNA concentration crossed set threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the cycle threshold (Ct) value for each gene from the raw PCR data.
outputs: Ct values for each gene in each sample

steps.description: The number was then normalized with 18S and referred to calibrator 450 ng Universal Human Reference RNA (Agilent Technologies).
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize Ct values using the Ct value of the 18S housekeeping gene (calculate dCt).

steps.description: The number was then normalized with 18S and referred to calibrator 450 ng Universal Human Reference RNA (Agilent Technologies).
steps.searchable_summary: Refer the normalized Ct values (dCt) to the calibrator sample (Universal Human Reference RNA) (calculate ddCt).
inputs: dCt values for the calibrator sample (Universal Human Reference RNA)

steps.description: Primary endpoint was Relative Quantification (RQ) calculated according to MIQE guidelines [30].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Relative Quantification (RQ) values from ddCt values according to MIQE guidelines (e.g., using 2^-ddCt method).
outputs: Relative Quantification (RQ) values for each gene in each sample

steps.description: All above-mentioned laboratory procedures were performed according to the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure all laboratory procedures follow manufacturer's instructions for kits and instruments.
inputs: Manufacturer instructions for kits and instruments
